seoTitle: Charleston vs Charlton Gully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Charleston and Charlton Gully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Charleston vs Charlton Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Charleston and Charlton Gully.
heading: Charleston vs Charlton Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Charleston, South Australia and Charlton Gully, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Charleston has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Charlton Gully (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Charleston or Charlton Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Charleston has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Charlton Gully scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Charleston scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Charlton Gully scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Charleston or Charlton Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Charleston has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
